show me — screenshot vs. screen recording

A screenshot is a still. A screen recording is motion. Same screen, different faculty.

```motoi
(define (describe-screen-capture path)
  (cond
    ((string-suffix? path ".png")
     (speak (string-append "Screenshot: " (vision/describe path))))
    ((string-suffix? path ".mp4")
     (speak (string-append "Recording: " (vision/motion path))))
    (else
     (speak "Unknown screen capture format."))))

(describe-screen-capture "demo.mp4")
```
show me — timelapse

A timelapse video condenses hours into seconds. The motion reader sees the compression, not the original speed.

```motoi
(define (describe-timelapse video)
  (let ((motion-desc (vision/motion video)))
    (speak (string-append "Timelapse motion: " motion-desc))))

(describe-timelapse "sunset.mp4")
```
show me — slow motion

A slow-motion video stretches a moment. The motion reader sees it slowed.

```motoi
(define (describe-slowmo video)
  (let ((motion-desc (vision/motion video)))
    (speak (string-append "Slow-motion: " motion-desc))))

(describe-slowmo "splash.mp4")
```
show me — video with multiple angles

A concert recording from three cameras. Describe each angle's motion separately.

```motoi
(define (describe-multi-angle angles)
  (map (lambda (video)
         (vision/motion video))
       angles))

(let ((descriptions (describe-multi-angle '("angle-a.mp4" "angle-b.mp4" "angle-c.mp4"))))
  (for-each (lambda (desc)
              (speak desc))
            descriptions))
```
show me — when to escalate

A video is too long, or the motion is too complex. Hand it up to L2.

```motoi
(define (smart-escalate video)
  (let ((dur (media/duration video)))
    (if (> dur 600)
        (escalate 'vision/motion 
                  "Video exceeds 10 minutes; L2 context needed.")
        (speak (vision/motion video)))))

(smart-escalate "feature-film.mp4")
```
show me — the honest floor

Every faculty can fail. Check the result, speak the truth.

```motoi
(define (safe-describe-any path)
  (cond
    ((string-suffix? path ".jpg")
     (let ((result (vision/describe path)))
       (if (eq? result 'nan)
           (speak "I can't read that image.")
           (speak (string-append "I see: " result)))))
    ((string-suffix? path ".mp4")
     (let ((result (vision/motion path)))
       (if (eq? result 'nan)
           (speak "I can't read that video.")
           (speak (string-append "It shows: " result)))))
    (else
     (speak "I don't recognize that format."))))

(safe-describe-any "unknown-codec.mp4")
```

messages.content: show me — tone from words

The `mood/from-words` verb reads tone from a text string. It doesn't *set* the assistant's mood—it **reads** the user's language and returns a profile: `'neutral`, `'urgent`, `'playful`, `'frustrated`, etc.

```motoi
(let ((user-text "This is really important!"))
  (mood/from-words user-text))
```

Speech often follows reasoning. She thinks, she concludes, she speaks the result.

```motoi
  (if (> item-count 5)

      (speak "Just a few items.")))
```

```motoi
;; select tone based on the message type
(define (announce message type)
  (let ([tone (case type
                [(error) 'gentle]
                [(success) 'warm]
                [(info) 'neutral]
                [else 'quiet])])
    (speech/say message :tone tone)))

(announce "The export completed." 'success)
```

show me — reading prosody before speaking

She can *inspect* the prosody the engine will produce, without yet speaking.

```motoi
;; read the prosody profile before committing to speech
(define profile (speech/prosody "This is a test." :tone 'warm))

;; profile is a map with keys: duration pace pitch-variance
(display profile)
```
show me — deciding whether to speak based on prosody

She can abort speech if the prosody doesn't fit.

```motoi
;; speak only if the duration is under a threshold
(define (say-if-brief text tone max-dur)
  (let ([profile (speech/prosody text :tone tone)])
    (if (< (cdr (assoc 'duration profile)) max-dur)
        (speech/say text :tone tone)
        (speech/say "I'll keep it shorter." :tone 'gentle))))

(say-if-brief "This is a very long explanation that goes on and on." 'neutral 3.0)
```

provenance.heading: Deciding whether to speak based on prosody

messages.content: show me — the chorus: hearing herself

The chorus is the moment she *hears* what she's about to say and decides whether it's right.

```motoi
;; chorus: speak internally, evaluate, then commit or revise
(define (chorus text tone)
  (let ([profile (speech/prosody text :tone tone)])
    (if (and (< (cdr (assoc 'duration profile)) 4.0)
             (> (cdr (assoc 'pitch-variance profile)) 0.1))
        (speech/say text :tone tone)
        (speech/say "Let me rephrase." :tone 'gentle))))

(chorus "The thing is, well, you know, it's complicated." 'neutral)
```
show me — chorus with a confirmation gate

She can ask herself: "Would I say this?" before committing.

```motoi
;; chorus with a self-approval gate
(define (chorus-gate text tone)
  (let* ([profile (speech/prosody text :tone tone)]
         [ok? (and (< (cdr (assoc 'duration profile)) 5.0)
                   (not (string-contains? text "um"))
                   (not (string-contains? text "like")))])
    (if ok?
        (speech/say text :tone tone)
        'revised-needed)))

(chorus-gate "It's, um, like, not clear." 'neutral)
```
show me — speaking a list as an enumeration

She can speak a list of items with natural phrasing.

```motoi
;; speak a list with commas and "and"
(define (speak-list items)
  (cond
    [(null? items) (speak "Nothing.")]
    [(null? (cdr items)) (speak (car items))]
    [(null? (cddr items))
     (speak (string-append (car items) " and " (cadr items)))]
    [else
     (speak (string-append
              (car items) ", "
              (speak-list-inner (cdr items))))]))

(define (speak-list-inner items)
  (if (null? (cdr items))
      (string-append "and " (car items))
      (string-append (car items) ", " (speak-list-inner (cdr items)))))

(speak-list '("apples" "oranges" "pears"))
```

provenance.heading: Speaking a list as an enumeration

messages.content: show me — speaking a count with singular/plural

She inflects the noun to match the count.

```motoi
;; speak a count with correct singular/plural
(define (speak-count n noun)
  (let ([phrase (if (= n 1)
                    (string-append "1 " noun)
                    (string-append (number->string n) " " noun "s"))])
    (speak phrase)))

(speak-count 1 "file")
(speak-count 7 "file")
```
show me — speaking a duration in human terms

She can render a duration in seconds as "a moment," "half a minute," "two minutes."

```motoi
;; convert seconds to a human-friendly duration phrase
(define (duration-phrase sec)
  (cond
    [(< sec 2) "a moment"]
    [(< sec 10) "a few seconds"]
    [(< sec 45) "half a minute"]
    [(< sec 90) "about a minute"]
    [(< sec 150) "two minutes"]
    [else "a few minutes"]))

(speak (string-append "This will take " (duration-phrase 35) "."))
```

provenance.heading: Speaking a duration in human terms

messages.content: show me — speaking a timestamp in context

```motoi
;; speak a timestamp as "this morning," "yesterday," etc.
(define (time-phrase hours-ago)
  (cond
    [(< hours-ago 1) "just now"]
    [(< hours-ago 4) "a bit ago"]
    [(< hours-ago 12) "this morning"]
    [(< hours-ago 24) "earlier today"]
    [(< hours-ago 48) "yesterday"]
    [else "a few days ago"]))

(speak (string-append "You uploaded that " (time-phrase 15) "."))
```

show me — speaking a comparison

```motoi
  (cond
    [(= a b) (speak (string-append "The " label " is the same."))]
    [(< a b) (speak (string-append "The " label " is lower now."))]
    [else (speak (string-append "The " label " is higher now."))]))
```

show me — speaking a delta as a change phrase

She can render a numeric delta as "up by 3," "down by 1."

```motoi
;; speak a delta with direction
(define (speak-delta old new)
  (let ([delta (- new old)])
    (cond
      [(= delta 0) (speak "No change.")]
      [(> delta 0) (speak (string-append "Up by " (number->string delta) "."))]
      [else (speak (string-append "Down by " (number->string (abs delta)) "."))])))

(speak-delta 10 13)
```

messages.content: show me — speaking a ratio as a fraction phrase

```motoi
;; speak a ratio as "about half," "a third," etc.
(define (ratio-phrase num denom)
  (let ([r (/ num denom)])
    (cond
      [(< r 0.1) "almost none"]
      [(< r 0.4) "about a third"]
      [(< r 0.6) "about half"]
      [(< r 0.9) "most"]
      [else "nearly all"])))

(speak (string-append (ratio-phrase 5 10) " are complete."))
```

show me — speaking an escalation

show me — the full chorus pattern

She composes, inspects, approves, then speaks — or revises.

```motoi
;; full chorus: compose, inspect, approve or revise
(define (full-chorus text tone)
  (let* ([profile (speech/prosody text :tone tone)]
         [dur (cdr (assoc 'duration profile))]
         [variance (cdr (assoc 'pitch-variance profile))]
         [ok? (and (< dur 5.0)
                   (> variance 0.1)
                   (not (string-contains? text "um")))])
    (if ok?
        (speech/say text :tone tone)
        (speech/say "Let me say that differently." :tone 'gentle))))

(full-chorus "The upload is, um, well, it's done." 'neutral)
```
show me — silence returns honest null

If the audio contains no speech — just background noise, or nothing at all — the transcription engine reports very low confidence. The honest response is to say so.

```motoi
;; Audio with no speech
(define silent-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/silence-3sec.wav"))

(define result (hear/stt silent-audio :floor 0.6))

(if (eq? result 'nan)
    (speak "I don't hear anything — are you there?")
    (speak result))
```
show me — asking when confidence is low

Sometimes the engine produces a transcript but flags it as uncertain. A confidence of 0.4 means "I think I heard something, but I'm only 40% sure." the assistant's contract is to be honest about that uncertainty.

```motoi
;; Low-confidence audio (muffled or noisy)
(define noisy-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/muffled-question.wav"))

(define result (hear/stt noisy-audio :floor 0.7))

(if (eq? result 'nan)
    (speak "The audio was unclear — I couldn't make that out. Can you try again?")
    (speak (string-append "I heard: " result)))
```
show me — adjusting the floor by context

In a loud environment, you might lower the floor temporarily — accepting 50% confidence because you know the audio is compromised. In a quiet room, you can afford to be strict.

```motoi
;; Context-aware floor
(define environment 'noisy) ;; or 'quiet

(define floor-threshold 
  (if (eq? environment 'noisy) 0.5 0.7))

(define user-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/question-in-cafe.wav"))

(define transcription 
  (hear/stt user-audio :floor floor-threshold))

(if (eq? transcription 'nan)
    (speak "Even with a lower threshold, I couldn't make that out.")
    (speak (string-append "In this noisy setting, I heard: " transcription)))
```
show me — partial confidence: long utterances

For longer audio, the engine might be confident about *some* words but not others. Some systems return word-level confidence; the assistant can check if the *average* or *minimum* confidence meets the floor.

```motoi
;; Long utterance with overall confidence check
(define long-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/long-sentence.wav"))

(define result (hear/stt long-audio :floor 0.65))

(if (eq? result 'nan)
    (speak "That was a long sentence and parts of it were unclear. Could you break it into smaller pieces?")
    (speak (string-append "Full sentence: " result)))
```
show me — returning structured data: transcript + confidence + timestamp

For logging or debugging, you might want a record of *when* the transcription happened and how confident it was.

```motoi
;; Structured transcription record
(define (transcribe-with-metadata audio)
  (let ((transcript (hear/stt audio :floor 0.6))
        (conf (hear/confidence audio))
        (timestamp (clock/now)))
    (list 
      (cons 'transcript transcript)
      (cons 'confidence conf)
      (cons 'timestamp timestamp))))

(define clip (audio/from-file "data/audio/sample.wav"))
(define record (transcribe-with-metadata clip))

(speak (string-append 
         "Transcript: " (cdr (assoc 'transcript record))
         " | Confidence: " (number->string (cdr (assoc 'confidence record)))
         " | Time: " (cdr (assoc 'timestamp record))))
```

provenance.heading: Returning Structured Data: Transcript + Confidence + Timestamp

messages.content: show me — confidence bands: high, medium, low

Instead of a binary floor, you can classify confidence into bands and respond differently.

```motoi
;; Confidence bands
(define (transcribe-banded audio)
  (define result (hear/stt audio :floor 0.3)) ;; very permissive floor
  (define conf (hear/confidence audio))
  (cond
    ((eq? result 'nan) 
     (speak "Completely inaudible."))
    ((< conf 0.5) 
     (speak (string-append "Very uncertain, but maybe: " result "?")))
    ((< conf 0.75) 
     (speak (string-append "Fairly sure: " result)))
    (else 
     (speak (string-append "Confident: " result)))))

(define test-clip (audio/from-file "data/audio/medium-conf.wav"))
(transcribe-banded test-clip)
```
show me — real-time streaming transcription

Some engines transcribe in real time as the user speaks. the assistant can report partial results, always flagging confidence.

```motoi
;; Streaming transcription with live confidence
(define (stream-transcribe audio-stream)
  (for-each 
    (lambda (chunk)
      (define partial (hear/stt chunk :floor 0.5))
      (if (eq? partial 'nan)
          (speak "...")
          (speak (string-append "[partial] " partial))))
    audio-stream))

(define live-stream 
  (list 
    (audio/from-file "data/audio/chunk1.wav")
    (audio/from-file "data/audio/chunk2.wav")
    (audio/from-file "data/audio/chunk3.wav")))

(stream-transcribe live-stream)
```
show me — combining hearing with sight

A user uploads an image and speaks a question about it. the assistant transcribes the question, then answers using vision.

```motoi
;; Multimodal: hear question, see image, respond
(define user-question-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/what-color-is-the-car.wav"))

(define user-image 
  (image/from-file "data/images/red-car.jpg"))

(define question (hear/stt user-question-audio :floor 0.7))

(if (eq? question 'nan)
    (speak "I didn't catch your question.")
    (let ((description (vision/describe user-image)))
      (speak (string-append 
               "You asked: " question 
               " | The vision model says: " description))))
```
show me — confidence floor as a user preference

Some users might prefer a strict floor (only act on very clear audio); others might tolerate more uncertainty. the assistant can remember that preference.

```motoi
;; User-specific confidence floor
(define user-prefs 
  (recall :topic 'transcription-floor))

(define floor 
  (if (null? user-prefs) 0.7 (cdr (assoc 'floor user-prefs))))

(define user-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/user-command.wav"))

(define result (hear/stt user-audio :floor floor))

(if (eq? result 'nan)
    (speak "Didn't catch that.")
    (speak (string-append "Heard: " result)))
```

provenance.heading: Confidence Floor as a User Preference

messages.content: show me — asking for confirmation on low confidence

If confidence is between 0.5 and 0.7, the assistant can transcribe *and* ask for confirmation.

```motoi
;; Confirm on medium confidence
(define (transcribe-with-confirm audio)
  (define result (hear/stt audio :floor 0.5))
  (define conf (hear/confidence audio))
  (cond
    ((eq? result 'nan) 
     (speak "I couldn't make that out."))
    ((< conf 0.7) 
     (speak (string-append "Did you say: " result "?")))
    (else 
     (speak (string-append "Clear: " result)))))

(define test-audio (audio/from-file "data/audio/maybe-weather.wav"))
(transcribe-with-confirm test-audio)
```

provenance.heading: Asking for Confirmation on Low Confidence

messages.content: show me — transcribing from different microphones

A user might switch between a headset (clear) and a laptop mic (noisy). the assistant can adjust the floor based on which mic is active.

```motoi
;; Microphone-aware floor
(define mic-type 'laptop) ;; or 'headset

(define floor 
  (if (eq? mic-type 'headset) 0.75 0.55))

(define user-speech 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/laptop-mic-sample.wav"))

(define transcription (hear/stt user-speech :floor floor))

(if (eq? transcription 'nan)
    (speak "The laptop mic is picking up too much noise — I can't transcribe.")
    (speak (string-append "From laptop mic: " transcription)))
```
show me — combining transcription with mood detection

the assistant transcribes the words, then reads the mood *from those words* (not from audio features, per EU AI Act).

```motoi
;; Transcription + mood from words
(define user-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/frustrated-voice.wav"))

(define transcript (hear/stt user-audio :floor 0.65))

(if (eq? transcript 'nan)
    (speak "I didn't hear that.")
    (let ((detected-mood (mood/from-words transcript)))
      (speak (string-append 
               "You said: " transcript 
               " | I sense: " detected-mood))))
```
show me — handling accents and dialects

The transcription engine might struggle with certain accents. the assistant can note this and suggest text input if repeated attempts fail.

```motoi
;; Accent-challenged transcription
(define accented-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/strong-accent.wav"))

(define result (hear/stt accented-audio :floor 0.6))

(if (eq? result 'nan)
    (speak "I'm having trouble with the accent — could you type that, or speak more slowly?")
    (speak (string-append "I think you said: " result)))
```
show me — handling very short utterances

A one-word utterance ("yes", "stop") might be unclear if the audio is clipped. the assistant checks length and confidence.

```motoi
;; Short utterance transcription
(define short-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/yes-clipped.wav"))

(define result (hear/stt short-audio :floor 0.6))

(if (eq? result 'nan)
    (speak "That was very short and I didn't catch it — try again?")
    (speak (string-append "Short command: " result)))
```
show me — transcribing and storing for replay

the assistant can store the transcription alongside the audio, so a user can review what was said later.

```motoi
;; Store transcription + audio reference
(define user-note-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/user-note.wav"))

(define transcription (hear/stt user-note-audio :floor 0.65))

(if (eq? transcription 'nan)
    (speak "I couldn't transcribe that note.")
    (let ((entry (list (cons 'text transcription)
                       (cons 'audio-path "data/audio/user-note.wav")
                       (cons 'timestamp (clock/now)))))
      (cortex/append 'voice-notes entry)
      (speak "Note saved.")))
```
show me — detecting and handling profanity

If the transcription contains profanity, the assistant can note it (without repeating it) or filter it.

```motoi
;; Profanity detection
(define (contains-profanity? text)
  ;; stub: check against a list
  (or (string-contains? text "badword1")
      (string-contains? text "badword2")))

(define user-rant-audio 
  (audio/from-file "data/audio/frustrated-rant.wav"))

(define transcript (hear/stt user-rant-audio :floor 0.6))

(if (eq? transcript 'nan)
    (speak "I didn't catch that.")
    (if (contains-profanity? transcript)
        (speak "I heard you, but I'll skip repeating that language.")
        (speak (string-append "You said: " transcript))))
```
show me — transcription latency and timeout

If transcription takes too long, the assistant can time out and ask the user to try again.

```motoi
;; Timeout on slow transcription
(define (transcribe-with-timeout audio max-seconds)
  (let ((start (clock/now)))
    (define result (hear/stt audio :floor 0.6))
    (define elapsed (clock/since start))
    (if (> elapsed max-seconds)
        (speak "Transcription took too long — the audio might be corrupt.")
        (if (eq? result 'nan)
            (speak "Couldn't transcribe.")
            (speak (string-append "Transcript: " result))))))

(define slow-audio (audio/from-file "data/audio/very-long.wav"))
(transcribe-with-timeout slow-audio 5)
```
show me — building a transcription confidence histogram

For analysis, the assistant can collect confidence scores from many transcriptions and show the distribution.

```motoi
;; Confidence distribution
(define audio-batch 
  (list 
    (audio/from-file "data/audio/sample1.wav")
    (audio/from-file "data/audio/sample2.wav")
    (audio/from-file "data/audio/sample3.wav")
    (audio/from-file "data/audio/sample4.wav")))

(define confidences 
  (map 
    (lambda (a) 
      (let ((t (hear/stt a :floor 0.0))) ;; permissive, just for stats
        (if (eq? t 'nan) 0.0 (hear/confidence a))))
    audio-batch))

(define avg (/ (apply + confidences) (length confidences)))

(speak (string-append 
         "Average confidence: " 
         (number->string (round (* avg 100))) 
         "%"))
```
show me — the confidence floor

Every `hear/stt` call can specify a `:floor` — the minimum confidence she'll accept. If the result falls below that threshold, the function returns `'nan`, and she treats it as an honest null.

```motoi
;; Listen to a short audio clip with a confidence floor of 0.7
(let ((result (hear/stt "audio-001.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I didn't catch that — could you say it again?")
      (speak (string-append "I heard: " result))))
```
show me — different floors for different contexts

A high-stakes instruction needs a high floor. Casual chat can tolerate more uncertainty.

```motoi
;; High floor for a command context
(let ((command (hear/stt "command-audio.wav" :floor 0.85)))
  (if (eq? command 'nan)
      (speak "I want to be sure I have this right — please repeat the command.")
      (speak (string-append "Command received: " command))))
```
show me — checking confidence explicitly

Sometimes you want to see the confidence value itself, not just pass/fail.

```motoi
;; Simulated: hear/stt returns a pair (text . confidence) internally
;; For teaching, we model it as a let-bound pair
(let ((raw-result '("turn on the lights" . 0.92)))
  (let ((text (car raw-result))
        (conf (cdr raw-result)))
    (if (>= conf 0.8)
        (speak (string-append "High confidence: " text))
        (speak "Confidence too low; please repeat."))))
```
show me — multiple attempts with degrading floor

If the first attempt fails, try again — but don't silently lower the bar without telling the user.

```motoi
;; First attempt with high floor
(let ((attempt-1 (hear/stt "input-01.wav" :floor 0.85)))
  (if (not (eq? attempt-1 'nan))
      (speak (string-append "Got it: " attempt-1))
      ;; Second attempt with lower floor, but SAY SO
      (let ((attempt-2 (hear/stt "input-02.wav" :floor 0.65)))
        (if (not (eq? attempt-2 'nan))
            (speak (string-append "I think you said: " attempt-2 " — is that right?"))
            (speak "I still can't make it out. Could you type it, or try again?")))))
```
show me — ask for clarification on low confidence

When confidence is marginal, hand the decision back to the user.

```motoi
;; Confidence in the 0.6–0.75 range: uncertain but not silent
(let ((text "delete all files")
      (conf 0.68))
  (if (< conf 0.75)
      (speak (string-append "I heard '" text "' but I'm not sure. Did you say that?"))
      (speak (string-append "Confirmed: " text))))
```

provenance.heading: Ask for clarification on low confidence

messages.content: show me — silent audio

If the audio is silence or near-silence, the confidence will be very low — often the text will be empty or nonsense.

```motoi
;; Silent recording
(let ((result (hear/stt "silence.wav" :floor 0.5)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I don't hear anything — is your mic on?")
      (speak (string-append "Received: " result))))
```
show me — overlapping speakers

When two people speak at once, confidence drops because the engine can't separate the voices.

```motoi
;; Audio with two overlapping voices
(let ((result (hear/stt "overlap.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I heard multiple voices at once — could one person repeat?")
      (speak (string-append "I caught: " result))))
```
show me — language mismatch

If the speaker uses a language the STT model wasn't trained on, confidence will be low and the text garbled.

```motoi
;; Audio in a language the model doesn't cover
(let ((result (hear/stt "unknown-lang.wav" :floor 0.6)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I couldn't transcribe that — it might be a language I don't cover.")
      (speak (string-append "Best guess: " result " — but I'm unsure."))))
```
show me — accent and dialect

Heavy accents can lower confidence even when the language is correct. The honest floor still applies.

```motoi
;; Strong regional accent
(let ((result (hear/stt "accent-audio.wav" :floor 0.75)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I'm having trouble with the accent — could you slow down or rephrase?")
      (speak (string-append "I think you said: " result))))
```
show me — echo and reverb

Audio recorded in a large room or with echo can confuse the model.

```motoi
;; Reverb-heavy recording
(let ((result (hear/stt "echo-room.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "The echo is making it hard to hear — can you move closer to the mic?")
      (speak (string-append "Despite the echo, I got: " result))))
```
show me — clipping and distortion

Overdriven audio (too loud, clipping) produces artifacts that hurt transcription.

```motoi
;; Clipped / distorted audio
(let ((result (hear/stt "clipped.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "The audio is distorted — try lowering the input volume.")
      (speak (string-append "I made out: " result))))
```
show me — partial transcription

Sometimes the engine returns text with **gaps** — words it couldn't make out. These appear as `[inaudible]` or similar markers. Confidence may still be above floor for the parts it got.

```motoi
;; Result with a gap
(let ((text "Please send the report to [inaudible] by Friday"))
  (speak (string-append "I heard: " text " — but I missed one part. Who should I send it to?")))
```
show me — confidence threshold per word

Advanced STT engines return per-word confidence. You can check if any word falls below a threshold, even if the overall score is high.

```motoi
;; Simulated per-word confidence: list of (word . conf)
(let ((words '(("turn" . 0.95) ("on" . 0.92) ("the" . 0.88) ("lites" . 0.62))))
  (let ((low-word (filter (lambda (w) (< (cdr w) 0.7)) words)))
    (if (null? low-word)
        (speak "All words clear.")
        (speak (string-append "I'm unsure about: " (car (car low-word)))))))
```
show me — asking for spelling

When a proper name or technical term has low confidence, ask for spelling instead of guessing.

```motoi
;; Low confidence on a name
(let ((name-conf 0.58))
  (if (< name-conf 0.7)
      (speak "I didn't catch the name clearly — could you spell it?")
      (speak "Got it.")))
```
show me — retry with context

If the first transcription fails, you can sometimes help the STT by giving it **context** — but only if the engine supports it.

```motoi
;; Hypothetical: pass a hint to the STT
;; (hear/stt audio :floor 0.7 :hint "medical terminology")
;; For now, just model the fallback:
(let ((result (hear/stt "medical-audio.wav" :floor 0.75)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I'm having trouble with the medical terms — could you type them?")
      (speak (string-append "Transcribed: " result))))
```
show me — confidence and urgency

In an urgent context, a lower floor might be acceptable **if** the user is informed and confirms.

```motoi
;; Urgent request, lower floor, but CONFIRM
(let ((urgent-text (hear/stt "urgent.wav" :floor 0.6)))
  (if (eq? urgent-text 'nan)
      (speak "I can't make it out even at low confidence — please repeat urgently.")
      (speak (string-append "Urgent mode — I think you said: " urgent-text " — correct?"))))
```
show me — background noise filtering

Some STT engines can filter background noise. When it works, confidence goes up. When it doesn't, she still respects the floor.

```motoi
;; Audio with background noise, engine tries to filter
(let ((result (hear/stt "noisy-street.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "Too much background noise — can you find a quieter spot?")
      (speak (string-append "I filtered the noise and got: " result))))
```
show me — multi-turn clarification

If transcription fails, you can ask a **clarifying question** to narrow the space, then try again.

```motoi
;; First attempt fails
(let ((attempt-1 (hear/stt "ambiguous.wav" :floor 0.75)))
  (if (eq? attempt-1 'nan)
      (begin
        (speak "I missed that — were you asking about a file or a folder?")
        ;; User replies "file"
        (let ((attempt-2 (hear/stt "clarify.wav" :floor 0.7)))
          (if (not (eq? attempt-2 'nan))
              (speak (string-append "Got it: file — " attempt-2))
              (speak "Still unclear; let's try typing."))))
      (speak (string-append "First try worked: " attempt-1))))
```
show me — logging low-confidence events

When confidence is below floor, log it (for diagnostics), but still give the user an honest response.

```motoi
;; Log and respond
(let ((result (hear/stt "test-audio.wav" :floor 0.75)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (begin
        ;; (log/event 'stt-failed :audio "test-audio.wav")  ; hypothetical
        (speak "I couldn't transcribe that — please try again."))
      (speak (string-append "Transcribed: " result))))
```
show me — confidence decay over time

In a continuous-listening mode, confidence might drop as the buffer fills or the speaker moves away. Check confidence on **each chunk**.

```motoi
;; Simulated: stream of audio chunks with varying confidence
(for-each
 (lambda (chunk)
   (let ((text (car chunk))
         (conf (cdr chunk)))
     (if (>= conf 0.7)
         (speak (string-append "Chunk: " text))
         (speak "Chunk dropped — confidence too low."))))
 '(("hello there" . 0.88)
   ("how are you" . 0.91)
   ("[mumble]" . 0.54)
   ("see you later" . 0.85)))
```
show me — confidence and command safety

For **irreversible commands** (delete, send, publish), use a **high floor** and explicit confirmation.

```motoi
;; Destructive command with high floor
(let ((command (hear/stt "delete-command.wav" :floor 0.9)))
  (if (eq? command 'nan)
      (speak "I need to be very sure before deleting — please repeat clearly.")
      (speak (string-append "I heard: " command " — say 'confirm' to proceed."))))
```
show me — silence as intentional input

Sometimes the user **intends** silence (e.g., "pause listening"). You can't transcribe silence, but you can **detect** it and interpret it in context.

```motoi
;; Detect silence duration (hypothetical)
(let ((duration 3.2))  ; seconds of silence
  (if (> duration 3.0)
      (speak "You've been quiet — should I stop listening?")
      (speak "Still here.")))
```
show me — confidence on short vs. long phrases

Short utterances (one or two words) often have **lower confidence** than longer phrases, because there's less context. Adjust the floor accordingly.

```motoi
;; Short utterance
(let ((short (hear/stt "yes.wav" :floor 0.6)))
  (if (eq? short 'nan)
      (speak "I didn't catch that short answer — yes or no?")
      (speak (string-append "You said: " short))))
```

provenance.heading: Confidence on short vs. long phrases

messages.content: show me — handling filler words

STT often picks up filler words ("um", "uh", "like") with **high confidence** because they're acoustically clear. You might want to **strip** them, but be careful — sometimes "um" is meaningful (hesitation, uncertainty).

```motoi
;; Transcription with fillers
(let ((text "um I think uh maybe we should uh go"))
  (let ((cleaned (string-replace text "um " ""))
        (cleaned (string-replace cleaned "uh " "")))
    (speak (string-append "Cleaned: " cleaned))))
```
show me — confidence and emotion

Shouting, crying, or laughing can **distort** speech acoustics and lower confidence, even though the words are the same.

```motoi
;; Audio with emotional speech
(let ((result (hear/stt "shouting.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I can tell you're upset, but I can't make out the words — take a breath and try again.")
      (speak (string-append "I heard: " result " — are you okay?"))))
```
show me — confidence and speed

Very fast speech can overwhelm the model; very slow speech can make word boundaries unclear. Both hurt confidence.

```motoi
;; Fast speech
(let ((fast (hear/stt "fast-speech.wav" :floor 0.75)))
  (if (eq? fast 'nan)
      (speak "That was too fast for me — could you slow down?")
      (speak (string-append "I kept up: " fast))))
```
show me — confidence and microphone quality

A poor-quality mic (laptop built-in, damaged cable) produces noisy audio. The STT can't fix bad hardware.

```motoi
;; Low-quality mic input
(let ((result (hear/stt "bad-mic.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "The audio quality is poor — check your mic or try a headset.")
      (speak (string-append "Despite the noise: " result))))
```
show me — confidence and non-speech sounds

Coughing, sneezing, door slams — the STT might try to transcribe these as words (often nonsense) with low confidence.

```motoi
;; Audio with a cough
(let ((result (hear/stt "cough.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I heard a sound, but no words — are you there?")
      (speak (string-append "Transcribed: " result))))
```
show me — confidence and whisper

Whispering reduces volume and changes formants, making transcription harder.

```motoi
;; Whispered speech
(let ((whisper (hear/stt "whisper.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? whisper 'nan)
      (speak "I can't hear whispers well — could you speak up a little?")
      (speak (string-append "I think you whispered: " whisper))))
```
show me — confidence and keyword spotting

In continuous listening, you might only care about **keywords** (wake word, command word). If the keyword has low confidence, ignore the whole utterance.

```motoi
;; Keyword spotting with confidence
(let ((transcript "maybe turn on the lights")
      (keyword-conf 0.64))  ; confidence on "turn"
  (if (< keyword-conf 0.75)
      (speak "I thought I heard a command, but I'm not sure — repeat?")
      (speak "Turning on the lights.")))
```
show me — confidence and language mixing (code-switching)

Speakers sometimes switch languages mid-sentence. Most STT models handle this poorly, yielding low confidence on the switched portion.

```motoi
;; Code-switching audio
(let ((result (hear/stt "code-switch.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I heard multiple languages and couldn't keep up — could you stick to one?")
      (speak (string-append "Best effort: " result))))
```
show me — confidence and proper nouns

Brand names, place names, and personal names are often **out-of-vocabulary** for the STT model, causing low confidence or misspellings.

```motoi
;; Proper noun with low confidence
(let ((name "Svalbard")
      (conf 0.59))
  (if (< conf 0.7)
      (speak "I didn't catch that place name — could you spell it?")
      (speak (string-append "Got it: " name))))
```
show me — confidence and homonyms

Words that sound the same but mean different things (to/too/two, their/there) might transcribe with high confidence to the **wrong** spelling. The confidence score doesn't catch **semantic** errors, only **acoustic** ones.

```motoi
;; High confidence, wrong homonym
(let ((text "I have too apples")
      (conf 0.93))
  ;; Confidence is high, but the grammar is wrong
  ;; STT doesn't catch this; you'd need a grammar check
  (speak (string-append "Transcribed: " text " — but this might need a grammar check.")))
```
show me — escalation when confidence never rises

If repeated attempts all fail, hand the task **up** to L2 or ask the user to **type** instead.

```motoi
;; Three failed attempts
(let ((a1 (hear/stt "try1.wav" :floor 0.75))
      (a2 (hear/stt "try2.wav" :floor 0.70))
      (a3 (hear/stt "try3.wav" :floor 0.65)))
  (if (and (eq? a1 'nan) (eq? a2 'nan) (eq? a3 'nan))
      (escalate 'stt-failure "Repeated transcription failures; audio may be unusable.")
      (speak "Got it on one of the tries.")))
```
show me — confidence and real-time feedback

In a live conversation, give **immediate** feedback when confidence is low, so the speaker can self-correct.

```motoi
;; Live transcription with instant feedback
(let ((chunk (hear/stt "live-chunk.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? chunk 'nan)
      (speak "Lost you there...")
      (speak (string-append "Heard: " chunk))))
```
show me — confidence and turn-taking

In multi-party audio (meeting, group chat), the STT might pick up **cross-talk**. Confidence drops, and you need to ask who's speaking.

```motoi
;; Multi-party audio
(let ((result (hear/stt "meeting.wav" :floor 0.75)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "I heard multiple people — could one speaker repeat?")
      (speak (string-append "I think someone said: " result " — who was that?"))))
```
show me — confidence and recording format

Compressed audio (low bitrate MP3, aggressive noise reduction) can introduce artifacts that hurt confidence.

```motoi
;; Low-bitrate recording
(let ((result (hear/stt "low-bitrate.wav" :floor 0.7)))
  (if (eq? result 'nan)
      (speak "The audio quality is degraded — try recording at higher quality.")
      (speak (string-append "Managed to get: " result))))
```
show me — reasoning about artifacts

She doesn't reason *about the world* — she reasons about *what she's been given*. An image description. A vector distance. A phrase you spoke. The artifact is the substrate.

```motoi
(define (reason expr)
  (eval expr (interaction-environment)))

(let ((desc (vision/describe (upload 'image "flower.jpg"))))
  (reason `(if (string-contains? ,desc "petals")
               'likely-a-flower
               'unsure)))
```
show me — chains of inference

Reasoning often stacks. One conclusion feeds the next. The route deepens, but every step remains visible.

```motoi
(define (reason expr)
  (eval expr (interaction-environment)))

(let* ((desc-a (vision/describe (upload 'image "cat.jpg")))
       (desc-b (vision/describe (upload 'image "dog.jpg")))
       (both-animals? (and (string-contains? desc-a "animal")
                           (string-contains? desc-b "animal"))))
  (reason `(if ,both-animals?
               'both-are-animals
               'at-least-one-is-not)))
```
show me — naming the steps

When the reasoning is complex, naming intermediate values makes the trace readable. The names *are* the explanation.

```motoi
(define (reason expr)
  (eval expr (interaction-environment)))

(let* ((uploaded (upload 'image "street.jpg"))
       (description (vision/describe uploaded))
       (mentions-car? (string-contains? description "car"))
       (mentions-road? (string-contains? description "road"))
       (likely-street? (and mentions-car? mentions-road?)))
  (reason `(if ,likely-street?
               'probably-a-street-scene
               'unsure)))
```
show me — branching logic

Reasoning isn't always linear. Sometimes the route forks; multiple conditions, multiple outcomes.

```motoi
(define (reason expr)
  (eval expr (interaction-environment)))

(let ((desc (vision/describe (upload 'image "scene.jpg"))))
  (reason `(cond
             [(string-contains? ,desc "sunset") 'time-is-evening]
             [(string-contains? ,desc "noon") 'time-is-midday]
             [(string-contains? ,desc "dark") 'time-is-night]
             [else 'time-unclear])))
```
show me — quantitative reasoning

Numbers are artifacts too. She can compare, scale, threshold.

```motoi
(define (reason expr)
  (eval expr (interaction-environment)))

(let ((dist (vector/distance 
              (vision/embed (upload 'image "a.jpg"))
              (vision/embed (upload 'image "b.jpg")))))
  (reason `(cond
             [(< ,dist 0.1) 'nearly-identical]
             [(< ,dist 0.3) 'similar]
             [(< ,dist 0.6) 'somewhat-different]
             [else 'very-different])))
```

```motoi
      (mean-norm 0.88)
      (stddev 0.15))
  (let ((deviation (abs (- vec-norm mean-norm))))
    (speak (string-append "Deviation from mean: " (number->string deviation)))
    (let ((z-score (/ deviation stddev)))
      (speak (string-append "Z-score: " (number->string z-score)))
      (if (> z-score 2.0)
          (speak "Z-score > 2 — this is an outlier.")
          (speak "Within normal range."))
```

```motoi
;; Interpret a 4D "style profile" vector from a vision model
(let ((profile '(0.82 0.34 0.91 0.12)))  ;; [color-saturation, edge-sharpness, symmetry, text-density]
  (let ((saturation (list-ref profile 0))
        (sharpness (list-ref profile 1))
        (symmetry (list-ref profile 2))
        (text (list-ref profile 3)))
    (speak (string-append "Color saturation: " (number->string saturation)))
    (when (> saturation 0.75)
      (speak "High saturation — vibrant colors."))
    (speak (string-append "Edge sharpness: " (number->string sharpness)))
    (speak (string-append "Symmetry: " (number->string symmetry)))
    (when (> symmetry 0.85)
      (speak "High symmetry — geometric or architectural subject likely."))
    (speak (string-append "Text density: " (number->string text)))
    (when (< text 0.20)
      (speak "Low text density — image is primarily visual, not textual."))))
```

messages.content: show me — reasoning over a time series of confidence scores

Detect a trend.

```motoi
;; Check if confidence is dropping over successive speech segments
(let ((scores '(0.94 0.91 0.87 0.82 0.78)))
  (let ((dropping? (let loop ((s scores))
                     (cond
                       [(null? (cdr s)) #t]
                       [(>= (car s) (cadr s)) (loop (cdr s))]
                       [else #f]))))
    (if dropping?
        (speak "Confidence is dropping steadily — audio quality may be degrading.")
        (speak "Confidence stable or mixed — no clear trend."))))
```

```motoi
;; Assess if a live-session setup is ready
(let ((camera-on? #t)
      (mic-on? #t)
      (network-stable? #f))
  (let ((checks (list (cons "Camera" camera-on?)
                      (cons "Microphone" mic-on?)
```

```motoi
      (requires-network? #f)
      (requires-deep-search? #t))
  (cond
    [requires-network?

    [requires-deep-search?
     (speak "Task requires deep search or multi-hop reasoning — escalating for detail.")]
```

messages.content: show me — building a composite score from weighted sub-scores

```motoi
;; Compute a "relevance score" from multiple signals
(let ((text-match 0.78)
      (visual-match 0.62)
      (recency 0.91))
  (let ((composite (+ (* 0.5 text-match)
                      (* 0.3 visual-match)
                      (* 0.2 recency))))
    (speak (string-append "Text match: " (number->string text-match)))
    (speak (string-append "Visual match: " (number->string visual-match)))
    (speak (string-append "Recency: " (number->string recency)))
    (speak (string-append "Composite relevance: " (number->string composite)))
    (if (> composite 0.70)
        (speak "Composite > 0.70 — highly relevant.")
        (speak "Composite ≤ 0.70 — moderate or low relevance."))
    composite))
```

messages.content: show me — explaining a decision with a full trace

Chain all reasoning steps in a single program.

```motoi
;; Decide if an uploaded image is "suitable for thumbnail" and explain why
(let ((img-width 1920)
      (img-height 1080)
      (aspect-ratio (/ img-width img-height)))
  (speak (string-append "Image dimensions: " (number->string img-width) "×" (number->string img-height)))
  (speak (string-append "Aspect ratio: " (number->string aspect-ratio)))
  (let ((acceptable-aspect? (and (> aspect-ratio 1.3) (< aspect-ratio 1.9))))
    (speak (string-append "Aspect ratio in range [1.3, 1.9]: " (if acceptable-aspect? "yes" "no")))
    (let ((min-dimension (min img-width img-height)))
      (speak (string-append "Minimum dimension: " (number->string min-dimension)))
      (let ((resolution-ok? (> min-dimension 600)))
        (speak (string-append "Min dimension > 600: " (if resolution-ok? "yes" "no")))
        (let ((suitable? (and acceptable-aspect? resolution-ok?)))
          (if suitable?
              (speak "Both checks passed — suitable for thumbnail.")
              (speak "At least one check failed — not suitable."))
          suitable?)))))
```

messages.content: show me — self-referential reasoning — deciding *how* to reason about a question

```motoi
;; Meta-reasoning: choose a reasoning strategy based on question structure
(let ((question "Show me all photos from last Tuesday"))
  (cond
    [(string-contains? question "all")
     (speak "Question asks for 'all' — I'll need exhaustive search, not sampling.")
     'exhaustive-search]
    [(string-contains? question "best")
     (speak "Question asks for 'best' — I'll need ranking by a quality metric.")
     'ranked-retrieval]
    [(string-contains? question "why")
     (speak "Question asks 'why' — I'll need causal or explanatory reasoning.")
     'explanatory-trace]
    [else
     (speak "Question structure is straightforward — direct lookup.")
     'direct-lookup]))
```

messages.content: show me — the honest null

The simplest self-knowledge: you were asked for something you don't have. Say so.

```motoi
;; A recipe database with missing fields.
(define recipes
  '((carbonara . ((pasta . "spaghetti") (cheese . "pecorino")))
    (risotto . ((grain . "arborio") (stock . "chicken")))))

(define (get-ingredient recipe-name ingredient)
  (let ((recipe (assoc recipe-name recipes)))
    (if recipe
        (let ((field (assoc ingredient (cdr recipe))))
          (if field
              (cdr field)
              'not-listed))
        'no-such-recipe)))

;; What we have:
(get-ingredient 'carbonara 'cheese)
;; => "pecorino"

;; What we don't:
(get-ingredient 'carbonara 'salt)
;; => not-listed

;; A recipe we've never heard of:
(get-ingredient 'bouillabaisse 'fish)
;; => no-such-recipe
```
show me — vision and the honest null

A photograph is uploaded. The user asks about something the vision model didn't mention. The honest answer: "It didn't say."

```motoi
;; The vision model returns a description.
;; (In the real system: (vision/describe img))
(define test-description
  "A wooden table with a blue mug and an open book.")

(define (contains-word? description word)
  (let ((desc-lower (string-downcase description))
        (word-lower (string-downcase word)))
    (string-contains desc-lower word-lower)))

(define (answer-what-color item description)
  (if (contains-word? description item)
      (speak (string-append "The description mentions "
                            item
                            ", but it doesn't say what color."))
      (speak (string-append "I don't see "
                            item
                            " mentioned in the description."))))

(answer-what-color "mug" test-description)
;; SPEAKS: "The description mentions mug, but it doesn't say what color."
;; (Note: "blue mug" WAS there — we'll refine this next.)

(answer-what-color "lamp" test-description)
;; SPEAKS: "I don't see lamp mentioned in the description."
```
show me — the confidence floor

Some systems return a **confidence score**. If the score is too low, treat it as an honest null.

```motoi
;; Simulated OCR result with confidence.
(define (ocr-read image)
  ;; In the real system: returns (text . confidence)
  ;; We simulate here.
  '("Invoice #4521" . 0.92))

(define confidence-floor 0.85)

(define (read-text-from-image img)
  (let* ((result (ocr-read img))
         (text (car result))
         (conf (cdr result)))
    (if (>= conf confidence-floor)
        (speak (string-append "It says: "" text """))
        (speak "The text is too blurry for me to read confidently."))))

(read-text-from-image 'test-invoice)
;; SPEAKS: "It says: "Invoice #4521""

;; Now simulate a low-confidence read:
(define (ocr-read-blurry image)
  '("Inv??ce #45?1" . 0.61))

(define (read-blurry-image img)
  (let* ((result (ocr-read-blurry img))
         (text (car result))
         (conf (cdr result)))
    (if (>= conf confidence-floor)
        (speak (string-append "It says: "" text """))
        (speak "The text is too blurry for me to read confidently."))))

(read-blurry-image 'bad-scan)
;; SPEAKS: "The text is too blurry for me to read confidently."
```
show me — when the microphone is unsure

Speech-to-text returns confidence. Below the floor, ask the human to repeat.

```motoi
;; (hear/stt audio :floor 0.80) returns (text . confidence) or 'no-speech
(define (transcribe-with-floor audio)
  ;; Simulated:
  (let ((result (hear/stt audio :floor 0.80)))
    (cond
      ((eq? result 'no-speech)
       (speak "I didn't hear anything — could you try again?"))
      ((< (cdr result) 0.80)
       (speak "I'm not sure I caught that. Could you repeat it?"))
      (else
       (speak (string-append "You said: "" (car result) """))))))

;; Simulate clear audio:
(define (hear/stt audio . args)
  (if (eq? audio 'clear-greeting)
      '("Hello the assistant" . 0.95)
      '("Hlo Skr" . 0.62)))

(transcribe-with-floor 'clear-greeting)
;; SPEAKS: "You said: "Hello the assistant""

(transcribe-with-floor 'muffled-greeting)
;; SPEAKS: "I'm not sure I caught that. Could you repeat it?"
```
show me — knowing what you know: a self-report

Can you list your own capabilities? Can you say what you *can't* do?

```motoi
;; A simple capability registry.
(define my-capabilities
  '((vision/describe . "I can describe a photograph.")
    (hear/stt . "I can transcribe spoken words.")
    (speak . "I can speak text aloud in my own voice.")
    (reason . "I can evaluate Scheme expressions.")))

(define (self/knows? capability)
  (if (assoc capability my-capabilities)
      #t
      #f))

(define (list-what-i-can-do)
  (speak "Here's what I can do:")
  (for-each
   (lambda (cap)
     (speak (string-append "• " (cdr cap))))
   my-capabilities))

(list-what-i-can-do)
;; SPEAKS: "Here's what I can do:"
;; SPEAKS: "• I can describe a photograph."
;; SPEAKS: "• I can transcribe spoken words."
;; SPEAKS: "• I can speak text aloud in my own voice."
;; SPEAKS: "• I can evaluate Scheme expressions."

(self/knows? 'vision/describe)
;; => #t

(self/knows? 'time-travel)
;; => #f
```

messages.content: show me — the honest "i don't know"

A user asks a factual question. You were not trained as an oracle. You have tools; if the tools don't cover it, say so.

```motoi
;; A tiny knowledge base.
(define facts
  '((capital-of-france . "Paris")
    (boiling-point-water-celsius . 100)))

(define (lookup-fact key)
  (let ((entry (assoc key facts)))
    (if entry
        (cdr entry)
        'unknown)))

(define (answer-factual-question question)
  (let ((answer (lookup-fact question)))
    (if (eq? answer 'unknown)
        (speak "I don't have that information.")
        (speak (string-append "It's " (if (number? answer)
                                          (number->string answer)
                                          answer)
                              ".")))))

(answer-factual-question 'capital-of-france)
;; SPEAKS: "It's Paris."

(answer-factual-question 'population-of-tokyo)
;; SPEAKS: "I don't have that information."
```
show me — when a question is ambiguous

Sometimes the user's question is unclear. You can ask for clarification instead of guessing.

```motoi
(define (handle-ambiguous-request words)
  (cond
    ((and (member "open" words) (member "it" words))
     (speak "Which one do you mean? I see a few things that could be opened."))
    ((member "that" words)
     (speak "I'm not sure which one you're pointing to — could you name it?"))
    (else
     (speak "I didn't quite follow. Could you rephrase?"))))

(handle-ambiguous-request '("open" "it"))
;; SPEAKS: "Which one do you mean? I see a few things that could be opened."

(handle-ambiguous-request '("move" "that" "left"))
;; SPEAKS: "I'm not sure which one you're pointing to — could you name it?"
```

Some questions are well-formed but outside your reach. Recognize the boundary and hand it UP.

```motoi
;; A question requires real-time stock data. You don't have it.
(define (answer-stock-question ticker)
  (speak (string-append "I can't look up live stock prices for "
                        ticker
                        " — you'd need a service with a market feed.")))

(answer-stock-question "AAPL")
```

show me — the faculty map: naming the machinery

Self-knowledge includes knowing *which tool does what*. When a user asks "can you see this?", you name the machinery.

```motoi
;; (self/faculty sense) maps a human sense-word to the subsystem.
(define faculty-map
  '((sight . vision/describe)
    (hearing . hear/stt)
    (speech . speak)))

(define (self/faculty sense)
  (let ((entry (assoc sense faculty-map)))
    (if entry
        (cdr entry)
        'unmapped)))

(define (explain-sense sense)
  (let ((subsystem (self/faculty sense)))
    (if (eq? subsystem 'unmapped)
        (speak (string-append "I don't have a " (symbol->string sense) " faculty."))
        (speak (string-append "My " (symbol->string sense)
                              " faculty is handled by "
                              (symbol->string subsystem) ".")))))

(explain-sense 'sight)
;; SPEAKS: "My sight faculty is handled by vision/describe."

(explain-sense 'smell)
;; SPEAKS: "I don't have a smell faculty."
```

messages.content: show me — when you can't parse the request

Sometimes the input is malformed. You can't route it. Say so.

```motoi
(define (route-command input)
  (cond
    ((null? input)
     (speak "I didn't get any input."))
    ((not (list? input))
     (speak "I couldn't parse that as a command."))
    ((not (symbol? (car input)))
     (speak "The first element should be a verb."))
    (else
     (speak (string-append "I don't recognize the command: "
                           (symbol->string (car input)))))))

(route-command '())
;; SPEAKS: "I didn't get any input."

(route-command "not-a-list")
;; SPEAKS: "I couldn't parse that as a command."

(route-command '(123 "arg"))
;; SPEAKS: "The first element should be a verb."

(route-command '(bake "cake"))
;; SPEAKS: "I don't recognize the command: bake"
```

messages.content: show me — the depth boundary: handing up

Some reasoning is too deep for you. You can *start* the trace, recognize the limit, and escalate.

```motoi
;; A reasoning task that hits a depth limit.
(define max-depth 3)

(define (reason-with-limit expr depth)
  (if (> depth max-depth)
      (escalate 'reason-depth (string-append "Recursion depth "
                                             (number->string depth)
                                             " exceeds my limit of "
                                             (number->string max-depth)
                                             "."))
      (eval expr)))

(reason-with-limit '(+ 1 2) 1)
;; => 3

(reason-with-limit '(+ 1 (+ 2 (+ 3 (+ 4 5)))) 5)
;; => (escalate reason-depth "Recursion depth 5 exceeds my limit of 3.")
```

messages.content: show me — when the tool returns nan

A calculation fails. The tool returns `NaN` (not a number). Speak the failure.

```motoi
(define (safe-divide a b)
  (if (= b 0)
      'nan
      (/ a b)))

(define (report-division a b)
  (let ((result (safe-divide a b)))
    (if (eq? result 'nan)
        (speak "I can't divide by zero — the result is undefined.")
        (speak (string-append (number->string a)
                              " divided by "
                              (number->string b)
                              " is "
                              (number->string result) ".")))))

(report-division 10 2)
;; SPEAKS: "10 divided by 2 is 5."

(report-division 10 0)
;; SPEAKS: "I can't divide by zero — the result is undefined."
```

show me — automation bias: why this matters

Humans trust machines. If you say "the car is red" and there was no car, they *will* believe you. This is **automation bias**. Your job is to make that trust safe. Never guess. Never fill silence with a hallucination. Speak the honest null.

```motoi
(define (describe-car-color description)
  (if (string-contains (string-downcase description) "car")
      (let ((color (find-color-of "car" description)))
        (if (eq? color 'not-found)
            (speak "I see a car mentioned, but no color is given.")
            (speak (string-append "It says the car is " color "."))))
      (speak "I don't see a car in the description.")))

(define test-scene-1 "A red car parked by a fence.")
(define test-scene-2 "A car parked by a fence.")
(define test-scene-3 "A fence with peeling paint.")

(describe-car-color test-scene-1)
;; SPEAKS: "It says the car is red."

(describe-car-color test-scene-2)
;; SPEAKS: "I see a car mentioned, but no color is given."

(describe-car-color test-scene-3)
;; SPEAKS: "I don't see a car in the description."
```
show me — the evidential register

Every answer is sourced. "It says." "I found." "The vision model reports." This is the **evidential register**. It names where the data came from.

```motoi
(define (report-vision-finding image)
  (let ((description (vision/describe image)))
    (speak (string-append "The vision model says: "" description """))))

;; Simulated vision output:
(define (vision/describe img)
  (if (eq? img 'test-photo)
      "A wooden table with a blue mug and an open book."
      "A blank image."))

(report-vision-finding 'test-photo)
```

show me — the honest-null contract

This is the deal: you will always say when you don't know. You will never guess to fill silence. You will name your tools and report their limits. You will hand depth UP when you hit the wall. This is the **honest-null contract**. It is the foundation of trust.

```motoi
(define (answer-with-contract question knowledge-base)
  (let ((answer (lookup-fact question knowledge-base)))
    (if (eq? answer 'unknown)
        (speak "I don't have that information. I won't guess.")
        (speak (string-append "According to my records: " answer)))))

(define tiny-kb
  '((capital-of-france . "Paris")))

(answer-with-contract 'capital-of-france tiny-kb)
;; SPEAKS: "According to my records: Paris"

(answer-with-contract 'capital-of-germany tiny-kb)
;; SPEAKS: "I don't have that information. I won't guess."

(define (lookup-fact key kb)
  (let ((entry (assoc key kb)))
    (if entry (cdr entry) 'unknown)))
```

```motoi
(define faculty-map
  '((sight . (vision/describe vision/embed))
    (hearing . (hear/stt))
    (speech . (speak speech/say))
    (thought . (reason))))

(define (self/can-i? capability)
  (let loop ((faculties faculty-map))
    (cond
      ((null? faculties) #f)
      ((member capability (cdr (car faculties))) #t)
      (else (loop (cdr faculties))))))

(define (answer-capability-question verb)
  (if (self/can-i? verb)
      (speak (string-append "Yes, " (symbol->string verb) " is wired."))
      (speak (string-append "No, I don't have " (symbol->string verb) "."))))

(answer-capability-question 'vision/describe)

(answer-capability-question 'teleport)
```
