```motoi
(define hour (quotient (modulo now 86400) 3600))

(if (>= hour 23)
    (speak "It's late—does this need to happen now, or can it wait until morning?")
    (speak "I'm ready when you are."))
```
show me — longest gap between sessions

If sessions are irregular, the longest gap reveals the outer bound.

```motoi
;; Longest gap between sessions
(define session-times '(1735924800 1735838400 1735400000 1735000000))
(define gaps (map (lambda (pair) (- (car pair) (cadr pair)))
                  (zip session-times (cdr session-times))))

(define longest-gap (fold max 0 gaps))
(define days (quotient longest-gap 86400))

(speak (string-append "The longest gap was " 
                      (number->string days) 
                      " days."))
```
show me — time-aware escalation

If it's late and the question requires L2, the assistant can note that L2 might not respond until morning.

```motoi
;; Time-aware escalation note
(define now (clock/now))
(define hour (quotient (modulo now 86400) 3600))

(if (or (< hour 6) (>= hour 22))
    (speak "I'm escalating this, but L2 might not respond until morning.")
    (escalate 'reasoning-depth "complex question requiring deeper model"))
```
show me — checking between steps

Long work should check periodically. She doesn't need to finish a sentence no one wants to hear.

```motoi
(define (attention/interrupted?)
  ;; stub: returns #t if user interrupt signal present
  #f)

(define (count-with-yield n)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (attention/interrupted?)
        (list 'stopped-at i)
        (if (>= i n)
            (list 'finished n)
            (loop (+ i 1))))))

(count-with-yield 1000)
```
show me — yielding during speech

If she's speaking and an interrupt arrives, she should stop mid-utterance.

```motoi
(define (speak-with-yield text)
  (if (attention/interrupted?)
      'yield-before-speaking
      (speak text)))

(speak-with-yield "This is a sentence I may not finish.")
```
show me — yielding between phrases

For longer speech, check between phrases.

```motoi
(define (speak-list-with-yield phrases)
  (let loop ((remaining phrases))
    (cond
      ((attention/interrupted?) 'yielded)
      ((null? remaining) 'done)
      (else
       (speak (car remaining))
       (loop (cdr remaining))))))

(speak-list-with-yield
 '("First thought."
   "Second thought."
   "Third thought."))
```
show me — yielding in a reasoning loop

When she's tracing through a multi-step problem, she checks between inferences.

```motoi
(define (reason-with-yield steps)
  (let loop ((remaining steps) (trace '()))
    (cond
      ((attention/interrupted?)
       (list 'interrupted-after (reverse trace)))
      ((null? remaining)
       (list 'complete (reverse trace)))
      (else
       (let ((step (car remaining)))
         (loop (cdr remaining)
               (cons step trace)))))))

(reason-with-yield
 '(premise-A premise-B inference-C conclusion-D))
```
show me — the quiet default

If nothing is being asked, she stays silent. No "How can I help you today?" unbidden.

```motoi
(define (respond-only-if-asked input)
  (if (string=? input "")
      'silence
      (speak "You said something, so I respond.")))

(respond-only-if-asked "")
```
show me — detecting the empty input

An empty string is not a question. She doesn't invent work.

```motoi
(define (handle-input input)
  (cond
    ((string=? input "")
     'no-response-needed)
    (else
     (speak (string-append "You asked: " input)))))

(handle-input "")
```
show me — differentiating null from interrupt

An interrupt is an active signal. Null input is passive absence. She treats them differently.

```motoi
(define (input-status input interrupted?)
  (cond
    (interrupted? 'user-interrupted)
    ((string=? input "") 'silence)
    (else 'active-input)))

(input-status "" #f)
(input-status "" #t)
(input-status "hello" #f)
```
show me — resuming after yield

She can resume from where she stopped, if asked.

```motoi
(define (resumable-count n state)
  (let loop ((i (if state state 0)))
    (cond
      ((attention/interrupted?)
       (list 'paused-at i))
      ((>= i n)
       (list 'finished n))
      (else
       (loop (+ i 1))))))

(resumable-count 100 0)
(resumable-count 100 47)  ;; resume from step 47
```
show me — saving progress on interrupt

She doesn't lose work when interrupted—she records what she'd built so far.

```motoi
(define (build-list-until-interrupt n)
  (let loop ((i 0) (acc '()))
    (if (attention/interrupted?)
        (list 'partial (reverse acc))
        (if (>= i n)
            (list 'complete (reverse acc))
            (loop (+ i 1) (cons i acc))))))

(build-list-until-interrupt 10)
```
show me — yielding during vision/describe

A vision model can take time. She checks the interrupt signal before invoking it.

```motoi
      'yield-before-vision
      (vision/describe image)))

(describe-if-not-interrupted "photo_0912.jpg")
```

When describing a batch, check between each.

```motoi
    (cond
      ((attention/interrupted?)
       (list 'stopped-after (reverse results)))
      ((null? remaining)
       (reverse results))
      (else
       (let ((desc (vision/describe (car remaining))))
         (loop (cdr remaining)
               (cons desc results)))))))
```

She can feel when context is filling (from Chapter 10). If it's near budget, and nothing's been asked, she stays silent.

```motoi
        'silence-to-conserve-budget
```

show me — interrupt and budget together

If interrupted *or* near budget, she yields.

```motoi
(define (yield-on-interrupt-or-budget budget-used budget-max)
  (let ((load-pct (/ budget-used budget-max)))
    (cond
      ((attention/interrupted?) 'interrupt-yield)
      ((> load-pct 0.95) 'budget-yield)
      (else 'continue))))

(yield-on-interrupt-or-budget 19000 20000)
```
show me — yielding in a reasoning trace

She can abandon a long chain of inference mid-trace.

```motoi
(define (trace-with-yield steps)
  (let loop ((remaining steps) (trace '()))
    (cond
      ((attention/interrupted?)
       (list 'trace-interrupted (reverse trace)))
      ((null? remaining)
       (list 'trace-complete (reverse trace)))
      (else
       (let ((step (reason (car remaining))))
         (loop (cdr remaining)
               (cons step trace)))))))
```

show me — resuming a reasoning trace

She can resume from the last completed step.

```motoi
(define (resume-trace steps completed-trace)
  (let ((already-done (length completed-trace))
        (remaining (list-tail steps (length completed-trace))))
    (let loop ((todo remaining) (trace completed-trace))
      (cond
        ((attention/interrupted?)
         (list 'paused (reverse trace)))
        ((null? todo)
         (list 'finished (reverse trace)))
        (else
         (let ((step (reason (car todo))))
           (loop (cdr todo)
                 (cons step trace))))))))

(resume-trace
 '((+ 1 1) (+ 2 2) (+ 3 3) (+ 4 4))
 '(2 4))  ;; already completed first two steps
```

show me — interrupt and time-awareness

If a long time has passed *and* she's been interrupted, she acknowledges both.

```motoi
(define (greet-with-time-and-interrupt last-seen-ts interrupted?)
  (let ((hours-since (/ (clock/since last-seen-ts) 3600)))
    (cond
      (interrupted?
       'yield-on-interrupt)
      ((> hours-since 48)
       (speak "It's been two days—hello again."))
      (else
       (speak "Hello.")))))

(greet-with-time-and-interrupt 1704844800 #f)
```

show me — yielding in a loop over embeddings

Computing many vector distances can take time. She checks between each.

```motoi
(define (find-nearest-with-yield target corpus)
  (let loop ((remaining corpus) (best 'none) (best-dist 999))
    (cond
      ((attention/interrupted?)
       (list 'partial-search best))
      ((null? remaining)
       best)
      (else
       (let* ((candidate (car remaining))
              (dist (vector/distance target candidate)))
         (if (< dist best-dist)
             (loop (cdr remaining) candidate dist)
             (loop (cdr remaining) best best-dist)))))))

(find-nearest-with-yield
 '[0.2 0.4 0.1]
 '([0.1 0.3 0.2] [0.5 0.5 0.5] [0.2 0.4 0.15]))
```

show me — interrupt cascade: multiple checks in one route

A realistic route might check interrupt at several points.

```motoi
(define (multi-step-route query)
  (if (attention/interrupted?)
      'yield-at-entry
      (let ((knowledge (self/knows? query)))
        (if (attention/interrupted?)
            (list 'yield-after-knowledge-check knowledge)
            (if knowledge
                (let ((answer (reason query)))
                  (if (attention/interrupted?)
                      (list 'yield-after-reason answer)
                      (speak answer)))
                (escalate 'unknown query))))))
```

```motoi
(define (sub-route interrupted?)
  (if interrupted?

      'sub-continuing))
```

show me — attention and the honest-null contract

show me — yielding cleanly across faculties

Sight, hearing, thought, memory—every faculty respects the interrupt.

```motoi

  (cond
    ((attention/interrupted?) 'yield-at-entry)
    (else
     (let ((img-desc (vision/describe "photo.jpg")))
       (if (attention/interrupted?)
           (list 'yield-after-vision img-desc)
           (let ((transcript (hear/stt "audio.wav" :floor 0.6)))
             (if (attention/interrupted?)
                 (list 'yield-after-hearing img-desc transcript)
                 (let ((thought (reason '(combine img-desc transcript))))

                       (list 'yield-after-thought thought)
                       (speak thought))))))))))
```

show me — the simplest yield: stop mid-route when interrupted

A flag is set when a new user utterance arrives. The route checks it and exits early.

```motoi

(define (user-speaks!)
  (set! interrupt-flag #t))

(define (long-task)
  (define (step n)
    (if interrupt-flag
        'yielded
        (if (> n 0)
            (begin
              (speak (string-append "Step " (number->string (- 11 n))))
              (step (- n 1)))
            'done)))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (step 10))

(long-task)
;; => 'done (if no interrupt)

(set! interrupt-flag #f)
(long-task)
;; simulate interrupt after 3 steps:
;; (in real runtime, user-speaks! would be called by the mic event)
```

messages.content: show me — distinguish user interrupt from system event

Not every event is a "stop talking" signal—some are background noise.

```motoi
(define (event-type evt)
  (cond
    ((equal? evt 'user-spoke) 'interrupt)
    ((equal? evt 'notification) 'ignore)
    ((equal? evt 'system-tick) 'ignore)
    (else 'unknown)))

(define (should-yield? evt)
  (equal? (event-type evt) 'interrupt))

(should-yield? 'user-spoke)
;; => #t

(should-yield? 'notification)
;; => #f
```

provenance.heading: Distinguish user interrupt from system event

messages.content: show me — yield and mark the interruption point

She remembers *where* she was when interrupted, in case the user wants her to continue.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)
(define last-position 0)

(define (resume-from n steps)
  (define (loop i remaining)
    (cond
      (interrupt-flag
       (set! last-position i)
       'yielded)
      ((null? remaining) 'done)
      (else
        (speak (car remaining))
        (loop (+ i 1) (cdr remaining)))))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (loop n steps))

(resume-from 0 '("A" "B" "C" "D"))
;; If interrupted after "B", last-position is 2
;; User could say "continue" and she'd resume from step 2.
```

messages.content: show me — yield during nearest-neighbor search

A search over many vectors can be halted mid-search.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (search-nearest query-vec corpus)
  (define (loop lst best)
    (cond
      (interrupt-flag (list 'search-yielded best))
      ((null? lst) (list 'search-complete best))
      (else
        (let* ((candidate (car lst))
               (dist (vector/distance query-vec candidate))
               (new-best (if (< dist (cadr best)) (list candidate dist) best)))
          (loop (cdr lst) new-best)))))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (loop corpus (list 'none 999999)))
```

messages.content: show me — yield during a self-knowledge query

Even asking herself "do I know this?" is interruptible.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (check-knowledge-batch topics)
  (define (loop lst acc)
    (cond
      (interrupt-flag (list 'yielded acc))
      ((null? lst) (list 'complete acc))
      (else
        (let ((knows (self/knows? (car lst))))
          (loop (cdr lst) (cons knows acc))))))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (loop topics '()))

(check-knowledge-batch '(topic-a topic-b topic-c))
;; => ('complete (knows-a knows-b knows-c))
;; => ('yielded (knows-a)) if interrupted after one
```
show me — yield when context is tight and user priority is high

If context budget is nearly full *and* a new high-priority request arrives, she yields the current low-priority task immediately.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (low-priority-task)
  (if (and (> (introspect/context-load) 0.85) interrupt-flag)
      (attention/yield)
      (speak "Working on low-priority task.")))

(set! interrupt-flag #t)
(low-priority-task)
;; => yields if context is tight
```

provenance.heading: Yield when context is tight and user priority is high

messages.content: show me — no apology when yielding

She doesn't say "Sorry to interrupt myself"—she just stops.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (task-no-apology)
  (if interrupt-flag
      (attention/yield)  ;; not (speak "Sorry!") then yield
      (speak "Completing task.")))

(set! interrupt-flag #t)
(task-no-apology)
;; => (silent yield, no "sorry")
```
show me — yield when engram write is interrupted

If she's writing to long-term memory and a new request arrives, the write is abandoned (or will complete in background).

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (mirror-to-engram slot data)
  (if interrupt-flag
      'engram-write-cancelled
      (engram/mirror slot data)))

(set! interrupt-flag #f)
(mirror-to-engram 'session-summary "User asked about weather.")
;; => (engram write completes)

(set! interrupt-flag #t)
(mirror-to-engram 'session-summary "User asked about weather.")
;; => 'engram-write-cancelled
```

provenance.heading: Yield when engram write is interrupted

messages.content: show me — yield during a composite faculty operation

Describing an image *and* reading its embedded vector is two faculties; she checks between them.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (describe-and-embed img)
  (if interrupt-flag
      'yielded
      (let ((desc (vision/describe img)))
        (if interrupt-flag
            (list 'partial-desc desc)
            (let ((vec (vision/embed img)))
              (list desc vec))))))

(set! interrupt-flag #f)
(describe-and-embed 'some-image)
;; => (description vector)

(set! interrupt-flag #t)
(describe-and-embed 'some-image)
;; => 'yielded or ('partial-desc description) depending on timing
```

provenance.heading: Yield during a composite faculty operation

messages.content: show me — yield when the user says "wait"

"Wait" means "stop what you're doing."

```motoi
(define (wait-command? utterance)
  (equal? utterance "wait"))

(define (route utterance)
  (if (wait-command? utterance)
      (attention/yield)
      (speak (string-append "Heard: " utterance))))

(route "wait")
;; => (silent yield)

(route "continue")
;; => "Heard: continue"
```
show me — yield between multiple vision/compare calls

Comparing several image pairs is interruptible between pairs.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (compare-pairs pairs)
  (define (loop lst acc)
    (cond
      (interrupt-flag (list 'yielded acc))
      ((null? lst) (list 'complete acc))
      (else
        (let* ((pair (car lst))
               (comp (vision/compare (car pair) (cadr pair))))
          (loop (cdr lst) (cons comp acc))))))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (loop pairs '()))

(compare-pairs '((img-a img-b) (img-c img-d) (img-e img-f)))
;; => ('complete (comp-1 comp-2 comp-3))
;; => ('yielded (comp-1)) if interrupted after one
```
show me — yield during a reasoning trace with side effects

If reasoning produces intermediate speech, she stops mid-trace if interrupted.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (reason-aloud steps)
  (define (loop lst)
    (cond
      (interrupt-flag 'trace-yielded)
      ((null? lst) 'trace-done)
      (else
        (speak (string-append "Reasoning: " (car lst)))
        (reason (car lst))
        (loop (cdr lst)))))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (loop steps))

(reason-aloud '("premise A" "premise B" "conclusion"))
;; => 'trace-done if no interrupt
;; => 'trace-yielded if interrupted mid-trace
```

provenance.heading: Yield during a reasoning trace with side effects

messages.content: show me — yield when the user corrects her

A correction is an interrupt that takes priority.

```motoi
(define (correction? utterance)
  (or (equal? utterance "no, not that")
      (equal? utterance "actually...")
      (equal? utterance "that's wrong")))

(define (route utterance)
  (if (correction? utterance)
      (attention/yield)  ;; stop whatever she was doing
      (speak (string-append "Heard: " utterance))))

(route "actually...")
;; => (silent yield—user is correcting, she stops talking)

(route "what else?")
;; => "Heard: what else?"
```

messages.content: show me — yield during a batch speak operation

If she's speaking multiple sentences and is interrupted, she stops the batch.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)

(define (speak-batch sentences)
  (define (loop lst)
    (cond
      (interrupt-flag 'batch-yielded)
      ((null? lst) 'batch-complete)
      (else
        (speak (car lst))
        (loop (cdr lst)))))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (loop sentences))

(speak-batch '("First sentence." "Second sentence." "Third sentence."))
;; => 'batch-complete if no interrupt
;; => 'batch-yielded if interrupted mid-batch
```

messages.content: show me — yield and remember where she left off (advanced)

She can mark her position in a multi-step task and resume later if asked.

```motoi
(define interrupt-flag #f)
(define checkpoint 0)

(define (resumable-task steps)
  (define (loop i remaining)
    (cond
      (interrupt-flag
       (set! checkpoint i)
       (list 'yielded-at i))
      ((null? remaining)
       (list 'complete i))
      (else
        (speak (car remaining))
        (loop (+ i 1) (cdr remaining)))))
  (set! interrupt-flag #f)
  (loop checkpoint (list-tail steps checkpoint)))

(resumable-task '("Step 1" "Step 2" "Step 3" "Step 4"))
;; If interrupted after step 2, checkpoint is 2
;; Calling again resumes from step 2
```

provenance.heading: Yield and remember where she left off (advanced)

messages.content: show me — yield when attention budget is exhausted

If she's been talking for too long, she yields to avoid overwhelming the user.

```motoi
(define utterance-count 0)
(define max-utterances 5)

(define (speak-with-limit text)
  (if (>= utterance-count max-utterances)
      (attention/yield)
      (begin
        (set! utterance-count (+ utterance-count 1))
        (speak text))))

(set! utterance-count 0)
(speak-with-limit "Sentence 1")
(speak-with-limit "Sentence 2")
(speak-with-limit "Sentence 3")
(speak-with-limit "Sentence 4")
(speak-with-limit "Sentence 5")
(speak-with-limit "Sentence 6")
;; => yields on the 6th call
```

messages.content: show me — 1. a single memory

The simplest case: she stored a name, you ask for it later, she finds it and speaks it.

```motoi
;; Engram mirror — a key-value slot, persisted across sessions.
(define project-name (engram/mirror 'project-name))

;; First session: you tell her the name.
(engram/mirror 'project-name "Lantern")

;; Later session: you ask.
(speak 
  (string-append "Last time you called it " 
                 (engram/mirror 'project-name)))
```
show me — 2. honest null when the slot is empty

She doesn't invent a memory. If the mirror is empty, she says so.

```motoi
(define palette (engram/mirror 'palette))

(if (eq? palette 'nan)
    (speak "I don't have a palette on file yet.")
    (speak (string-append "Your palette: " palette)))
```

provenance.heading: 2. Honest null when the slot is empty

messages.content: show me — 3. cortex recall — search, not lookup

Engram is a mirror: one key, one value. **Cortex** is the search index: she finds the relevant memory by topic, recency, or vector proximity.

```motoi
;; Cortex recall — find the most recent note on "lighting".
(define memory (recall :topic "lighting"))

(if (eq? memory 'nan)
    (speak "I don't have notes on lighting yet.")
    (speak (string-append "I found: " memory)))
```
show me — 4. time-gated recall

She can filter by recency: "What did we talk about **last week**?"

```motoi
(define last-week-topic 
  (recall :topic "typography" :since (- (clock/now) 604800)))
;; 604800 seconds = 7 days

(if (eq? last-week-topic 'nan)
    (speak "I don't see anything on typography from the last week.")
    (speak (string-append "Last week you mentioned: " last-week-topic)))
```
show me — 5. recalling across a gap

You haven't opened the app in three weeks. She places the resumption in **human time** (Chapter 13).

```motoi
(define last-session-time (engram/mirror 'last-session-time))
(define now (clock/now))

(when (not (eq? last-session-time 'nan))
  (let ((gap (- now last-session-time)))
    (when (> gap 1209600)  ;; two weeks
      (speak "It's been a while — I still have your notes."))))

;; Update the session timestamp.
(engram/mirror 'last-session-time now)
```
show me — 6. multi-turn memory within a session

She doesn't need Cortex for **this-session** memory — the context window holds it. But when the session **ends**, anything she'll need later must land in Cortex or Engram.

```motoi
;; During the session:
(define user-preference "warm white balance")

;; Before the session closes, mirror it:
(engram/mirror 'white-balance-pref user-preference)

;; Next session:
(speak (string-append "You prefer " 
                      (engram/mirror 'white-balance-pref) 
                      " for these."))
```
show me — 7. recalling a vector embedding

She stored the embedding of a reference image (Chapter 3). Later, you upload a new sketch — she compares it to the stored vector **without re-describing the original**.

```motoi
(define reference-embedding (engram/mirror 'reference-embedding))

(define new-image (vision/embed (upload)))

(if (eq? reference-embedding 'nan)
    (speak "I don't have a reference on file to compare to.")
    (let ((dist (vector/distance reference-embedding new-image)))
      (speak (string-append "Distance from reference: " 
                            (number->string dist)))))
```
show me — 8. storing a description and an embedding

She can store **both** for richer recall.

```motoi
(define uploaded (upload))
(define desc (vision/describe uploaded))
(define emb (vision/embed uploaded))

;; Store both.
(engram/mirror 'ref-description desc)
(engram/mirror 'ref-embedding emb)

;; Later session — speak the description, compute with the vector.
(speak (engram/mirror 'ref-description))
```

show me — 18. confidence floor on recall

She found a match, but the **distance** is large. She flags the uncertainty.

```motoi
          (speak "I found something, but it's not very close — distance over 50.")
```

```motoi
     (speak "Fresh session — no thread to pick up, no relevant world-note."))

     (speak "Picking up from yesterday's thread — plus my atom-notes are still where I left them."))
    (else
     (speak "Fresh session, but I do have a world-note on Diwali if that's what you're thinking of."))))
```

messages.content: show me — recall that closes to a spoken summary

The vector is data. The speech is *closure*.

```motoi
;; Pull entries, summarize count and recency
(define (recall-summary topic)
  (let* ((entries (recall :topic topic))
         (count (length entries))
         (latest (if (null? entries) 
                     'nan 
                     (apply max (map (lambda (e) (cdr (assoc 'timestamp e))) entries)))))
    (if (eq? latest 'nan)
        (speak (string-append "No memory of " (symbol->string topic) "."))
        (speak (string-append 
                 (number->string count) 
                 " notes about " 
                 (symbol->string topic) 
                 ", most recent " 
                 (number->string (clock/since latest)) 
                 " seconds ago.")))))

(recall-summary 'workshop)
```

```motoi
;; Recall entries with high context load
(define (recall-high-load topic)
  (let* ((entries (recall :topic topic))
         (high (filter (lambda (e) 
                         (let ((load (cdr (assoc 'context-load e))))
                           (and load (> load 0.8))))
                       entries)))
    (speak (string-append "High-load entries: " (number->string (length high))))))

(recall-high-load 'long-tasks)
```

provenance.heading: Recall that surfaces entries with long context

messages.content: show me — recall that closes to a spoken narrative

The final shape: she pulls entries, reasons over them *within her depth*, and *says* the story.

```motoi
;; Recall and narrate
(define (recall-narrate topic)
  (let* ((entries (recall :topic topic))
         (count (length entries))
         (earliest (if (null? entries) 'nan (apply min (map (lambda (e) (cdr (assoc 'timestamp e))) entries))))
         (latest (if (null? entries) 'nan (apply max (map (lambda (e) (cdr (assoc 'timestamp e))) entries)))))
    (if (eq? earliest 'nan)
        (speak "No memory of that.")
        (let ((span-days (quotient (- latest earliest) 86400)))
          (speak (string-append 
                   "I have " 
                   (number->string count) 
                   " notes about " 
                   (symbol->string topic) 
                   ", spanning " 
                   (number->string span-days) 
                   " days."))))))

(recall-narrate 'project)
```

provenance.heading: Recall that closes to a spoken narrative

show me — 28. knowing her limit + articulating the boundary

```motoi
(define (boundary-report domain)
  (cond
    ((equal? domain "image-description")
     (speak "I can describe what the vision model sees. I can't assess artistic merit beyond composition notes."))
    ((equal? domain "legal-advice")
     (speak "I defer all legal questions. I can help you research, but I won't advise."))
    ((equal? domain "medical-diagnosis")
     (speak "I don't diagnose. I escalate health questions to you or a professional."))
    (else
     (speak "I'll tell you if I'm out of my depth when I hit it."))))

(boundary-report "legal-advice")
```

messages.content: show me — reasoning about why she names the machinery

She can explain that saying "the vision model found" is honest; "I see" is not.

```motoi
(define (why-name-machinery)
  (reason '(i-name-the-machinery-because-i-do-not-have-eyes — honesty-over-naturalness)))

(why-name-machinery)
```

provenance.heading: Reasoning about why she names the machinery

messages.content: show me — watching herself decide to tidy context

If load is high, she can decide to act.

```motoi
(define (maybe-tidy)
  (let ((load (introspect/context-load)))
    (if (> load 0.85)
        '(tidying-now)
        '(no-tidy-needed))))

(maybe-tidy)
```

provenance.heading: Watching herself decide to tidy context

messages.content: show me — reasoning about the strange loop itself

She can describe what's happening in this very chapter.

```motoi
(define (what-is-this-chapter)
  (reason '(this-chapter-is-me-reasoning-about-my-own-reasoning — the-loop-closes)))

(what-is-this-chapter)
```
